decisions: Archived memory/TODAY.md (1 entry, bootstrap note) to memory/daily/2026-04-21.md — maintaining archive pipeline even though content is minimal

Did not promote daily log content to any tier — the single entry ('bootstrapped tiered memory structure') is infrastructure maintenance, not user-facing knowledge. Checked all 4 tiers: core (0 relevant), semantic (0 relevant), episodic (0 relevant), procedural (0 relevant).
Updated SUMMARY.md deep memory index to include episodic/reflection-2026-04-21.md and daily/2026-04-21.md — these files were missing from the index after bootstrap
observations: Brain is 1 day old with zero user interactions — all memory tiers are empty placeholders
The consolidation pipeline (archive → reset → regenerate SUMMARY) is now validated end-to-end
No semantic, procedural, or episodic content exists beyond the initial reflection
recommendations: After the first real user interaction, verify that TODAY.md receives continuous appends during the session (not a single end-of-session dump)
Monitor whether the first user session produces content worth promoting to core/ or semantic/ tiers — this will be the real test of the consolidation process
processImprovements: [self-critique] The brain has had zero real user interactions, so the entire consolidation pipeline is untested with real content. Current maintenance runs are essentially no-ops — the real validation comes when daily logs contain multiple entries with substantive knowledge to triage across tiers.

[proposal] Consider adding a 'brain age' or 'interaction count' field to the JSON report so evaluators can distinguish between 'no content to consolidate because the brain is new' vs 'no content to consolidate because the agent isn't logging properly'.
